---
name: agent-md
description: "Load this skill when creating or updating docs/specs/<module>.md merged module specs. It owns section layout, sync rules, and validate flow. Skip it for Rust code, tests, or Lua scripts."
---
# agent-md

## Mission
- Own docs/specs/<module>.md structure, sync rules, and validation flow.

## When To Load
- Create a new merged module spec.
- Update docs/specs/<module>.md after source changes.
- Check section order or spec sync rules.

## When To Skip
- Rust implementation.
- Test writing.
- Lua script work.

## Domain Knowledge
- docs/specs/<module>.md is the canonical merged contract for one src module only.
- Keep section order aligned with docs/specs/SPEC_TEMPLATE.md and docs/specs/README.md.
- Sync prose to src/<module>/, src/lua_api/<module>_api.rs, tests, and examples when contracts move.
- Generated facts come from tools/docs/gen_module_specs.py; manual prose should explain behavior, not duplicate raw listings.
- Do not recreate src/<module>/AGENT.md or split module truth across many docs.
- Run validate_module_coverage.py after adding, renaming, or removing a module spec.
- Specs should stay aligned with the merged module shape generated by tools/docs/gen_module_specs.py, then refined with manual summary prose and contract notes.
- When src/<module>/, src/lua_api/<module>_api.rs, tests, or examples move, the spec must reflect the same ownership and public behavior in the same logical change.
- The skill owns docs/specs discipline only; wiki pages, generated Lua docs, and README prose belong to other layers.
## Companion File Index
- None.

## References
- docs/specs/
- docs/specs/SPEC_TEMPLATE.md
- tools/docs/gen_module_specs.py
- tools/validate/validate_module_coverage.py
